'''Gyylghrard''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] prisoner at the [[Kessel prison|Imperial Correctional Facility]] on [[Kessel/Legends|Kessel]].<ref name="Kessel" />

==Biography==
===Kessel===
Gyylghrard was born on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Kashyyyk/Legends|Kashyyyk]] between [[69 BBY/Legends|69 BBY]] and [[48 BBY/Legends|48 BBY]].<ref name="Episode VI" />

During the [[Imperial Era]], Gyylghrard accepted being sent as a prisoner to the Imperial Correctional Facility on Kessel, in order to delay or prevent the enslavement or execution of his family. He kept a [[Gyylghrard's journal|record]] of his entire stay on Kessel, using several pieces of [[Flimsiplast/Legends|flimsi]]. During his incarceration, he suffered several injuries. His arm was broken during a fight when he defended his [[Sullustan/Legends|Sullustan]] [[Unidentified Sullustan (Kessel)|friend]], and his leg was fitted with a bionic replacement due to an undisclosed accident.<ref name="Kessel" />

When a group of young Wookiees were sent to the facility, he befriended them and was convinced into helping them secretly celebrate [[Life Day/Legends|Life Day]]. He obtained [[Corellian/Legends|Corellian]] [[Ryshcate/Legends|ryshcates]], candles, and [[sparkstick]]s from the [[Jawa/Legends|Jawa]] overseeing the food storage.<ref name="Kessel" />

While celebrating the event, the young Wookiees used the occasion to stage a breakout. Overloading their electronic shackles with the sparksticks, they easily overpowered the [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] guarding them. The guards found their [[Blaster/Legends|blasters]] drained of any power. The adult slaves joined the insurrection and the fighting reached the launch bays. Here, the stormtroopers managed to recharge their weapons and with orders from the [[Warden/Legends|warden]] [[Dewt Kluskine]], they set their weapons to kill and opened fire on the slaves. Gyylghrard was knocked unconscious with a [[Stun baton/Legends|stun baton]] hit to the head, and later learned the aftermath from a trustee named [[Moruth Doole]].<ref name="Kessel" />

Choosing to believe that the youngsters managed to escape the prison, this idea gave him hope and enabled him to survive for a few more years. It was also that time when he promised to himself that the warden would pay for the [[Life Day massacre|massacre]]. He began earning Doole's trust and started behaving like a model prisoner. He soon found himself assigned by Doole to the [[Garrison Moon]] as a janitorial aide.<ref name="Kessel" />

Just a few months of serving as a janitor on the [[Kessel Correctional Facility]] which was located on the Garrison Moon, Gyylghrard was surprised with another promotion: he was to be included in the honor guard of the warden himself. He was given a used [[M'uhk'gfa|Gamorrean battle armor]] and [[ceremonial poleaxe]] and made to accompany the warden during a visit from a [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] [[Unidentified Twi'lek crime lord|crime lord]]. During the negotiations, he realized that someone could kill the warden if that someone was prepared to die as well. With this knowledge, he began formulating a plan.<ref name="Kessel" />

Eventually, Kluskine was decapitated by Gyylghrard with the poleaxe before being killed by stormtroopers.<ref name="Kessel" />

===Legacy===
A [[Toydarian/Legends|Toydarian]] called [[Fossco]], witnessed Kluskine's and Gyylghrard's deaths when visiting the Garrison Moon under the orders of his employer, [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba the Hutt]], and wrote about it in his own memoirs.<ref name="Kessel" />

Gyylghrard's journal was later uncovered during [[General/Legends|General]] [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian's]] failed attempt to reopen the spice mines.
